text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY / BONE MARROW PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Margaret Okonkwo
Medical Record Number: [MRN REDACTED]
Date of Birth: [DOB REDACTED]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: BMT/Hematology Attending
Clinical Service: Bone Marrow Transplant Program
Specimen Received: Bone marrow biopsy core and aspirate smears, plus peripheral blood
Specimen Laterality: Posterior superior iliac spine (right)
Clinical Indication: Day +30 post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) assessment; evaluation for engraftment, chimerism, and minimal residual disease (MRD) in a patient with history of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse-risk. Donor: 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated male donor; conditioning with fludarabine/IV busulfan (RIC); GVHD prophylaxis: tacrolimus/methotrexate/ATG.
Accession Number: [ACCESSION REDACTED]

---

SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION

Specimen A: Bone marrow core biopsy (right posterior superior iliac spine), decalcified formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue section; H&E stained slides x4; reticulin stain x1; immunohistochemical stains (CD34, CD117, MPO, CD3, CD20, glycophorin A) performed.
Specimen B: Bone marrow aspirate smears x8 (Wright-Giemsa stained); clot section x1.
Specimen C: Anticoagulated bone marrow aspirate, submitted for multiparameter flow cytometry (MRD panel), cytogenetics (conventional karyotype and FISH), STR-based donor chimerism analysis (whole marrow, T-cell subset, myeloid subset), and molecular studies (FLT3-ITD quantitative PCR, DNMT3A p.R882H digital droplet PCR).
Specimen D: Peripheral blood (EDTA), submitted for complete blood count differential and chimerism STR analysis (peripheral blood).

---

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE REDACTED]
TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Bone marrow core biopsy and aspirate (right PSIS), post-allogeneic transplant day +30 assessment

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY

Specimen A: Received in formalin is a bone marrow core biopsy measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The specimen is tan-white, firm, and cylindrical. Representative sections submitted in their entirety in one cassette following standard decalcification protocol (EDTA-based, 24 hours). Adequate specimen for histologic evaluation.

Specimen B: Received are eight bone marrow aspirate smears and one bone marrow clot section, all adequately labeled. The aspirate material appears adequate for morphologic evaluation. The smears are well-prepared with visible spicules and marrow particles.

Specimen C: Received in EDTA anticoagulated tubes (2 x 4 mL). Adequate viable cell count obtained for flow cytometric MRD panel, cytogenetic culture, chimerism STR analysis, and molecular studies. Cell viability: 94% by trypan blue exclusion.

Specimen D: Peripheral blood in EDTA tube, adequate for CBC and chimerism analysis.

---

DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

I. BONE MARROW CORE BIOPSY (Specimen A) - Histologic Examination

Overall cellularity: Normocellular to mildly hypercellular marrow for age, estimated at 50% overall cellularity. This represents a significant and expected increase compared to the hypocellular marrow (25% cellularity) documented at day +30 of induction (post-7+3 chemotherapy), and is now approaching normal reconstitution consistent with successful engraftment of donor hematopoietic stem cells.

Hematopoietic lineages:
- Erythropoiesis: Normal maturation with erythroid precursors present in appropriate numbers and distribution. No dysplastic nuclear changes (no nuclear irregularity, megaloblastoid change, or binucleation exceeding expected background). Glycophorin A immunostain highlights erythroid islands distributed throughout the biopsy.
- Granulopoiesis: Active myeloid reconstitution observed. Normal left-shifted myeloid maturation with promyelocytes, myelocytes, metamyelocytes, and band forms identifiable. No significant dysplasia. MPO immunostain highlights maturing myeloid elements in normal distribution.
- Megakaryopoiesis: Megakaryocytes present in normal to slightly reduced numbers (approximately 3-5 per 10x field). Morphology is unremarkable without significant hypolobation, micromegakaryocytes, or clustering abnormalities that would suggest residual dysplasia.
- Lymphocytes/Plasma cells: Small interstitial lymphocytes present in proportionate numbers, consistent with reconstituting post-transplant immune architecture. No lymphoid aggregates or sheets. Plasma cells comprise <2% of total cellularity.

Blast enumeration: CD34 immunostain performed. CD34-positive cells represent less than 2% of total nucleated marrow cells, with an estimated blast count of approximately 1-2% by morphology (normal range for a reconstituting marrow). Blasts are scattered and individually distributed without clustering, paratrabecular aggregation, or sheet-like arrangement. CD117 highlights occasional mast cells and rare CD117+ blasts, consistent with normal regenerative hematopoiesis. No Auer rods identified on core biopsy or aspirate smears.

Stromal changes: Trabecular bone is unremarkable. No edema, fibrosis, or necrosis identified. Reticulin stain: MF-0 (no reticulin fibrosis; resolved from MF-1 at diagnosis).

No morphologic evidence of residual or relapsed AML. No evidence of graft failure.

II. BONE MARROW ASPIRATE (Specimen B) - Morphologic Differential Count (500-cell manual differential)

Myeloblasts: 1.4%
Promyelocytes: 3.2%
Myelocytes: 12.4%
Metamyelocytes: 10.6%
Band neutrophils: 14.8%
Segmented neutrophils: 8.8%
Eosinophils (all stages): 2.2%
Basophils: 0.4%
Monocytes: 4.2%
Lymphocytes: 8.8%
Plasma cells: 1.2%
Erythroid precursors (all stages): 31.4%
Megakaryocytes: present, adequate (3-4/low power field)
M:E ratio: approximately 1.8:1 (normal)

Morphologic comment: Trilineage hematopoiesis with normal maturation is confirmed. The aspirate differential is within expected limits for reconstituting post-allogeneic transplant marrow. No dysplastic changes of diagnostic significance are identified. No Auer rods are seen. No morphologically abnormal blasts or promyelocytes suggestive of leukemic relapse.

III. PERIPHERAL BLOOD (Specimen D)

WBC: 3,600/uL; Hemoglobin: 10.4 g/dL; Hematocrit: 31.2%; MCV: 94 fL; Platelets: 98,000/uL
Differential: Neutrophils 64% (ANC 2,304/uL), Lymphocytes 22%, Monocytes 10%, Eosinophils 3%, Basophils 1%
Blast count on peripheral smear: 0% (no circulating blasts identified)
Red cell morphology: Mild normochromic normocytic anemia. No schistocytes. No teardrop cells. No dysplastic forms.
Platelets: Mildly reduced, morphologically unremarkable.
Comment: Peripheral blood findings consistent with early engraftment with ongoing hematopoietic recovery. No evidence of circulating leukemic blasts.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

IV. MULTIPARAMETER FLOW CYTOMETRY - MRD ASSESSMENT (Specimen C)

Method: 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry using a leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP)-based MRD detection strategy. A minimum of 500,000 viable events acquired. Panel components: CD45, CD34, CD117, CD13, CD33, HLA-DR, CD7, CD123, CD19, CD56, MPO (intracellular), CD3 (T-cell gating), CD14 (monocyte gating).

Result: NO LEUKEMIA-ASSOCIATED IMMUNOPHENOTYPE DETECTED.

The CD34+ compartment comprises approximately 0.08% of viable CD45+ cells (within normal regenerating marrow range). The CD34+CD117+ blast population does not exhibit the aberrant co-expression pattern identified at diagnosis (specifically, loss of the aberrant CD7+/CD123+ co-expression on CD34+ blasts that defined the leukemic LAIP). Normal antigen expression intensity and distribution is observed on all evaluated progenitor subsets.

Sensitivity threshold: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 cells). No aberrant population detected above this threshold.

MRD RESULT: NEGATIVE

V. CONVENTIONAL CYTOGENETICS - KARYOTYPE (Specimen C)

Method: GTL-banded metaphase spreads; minimum 20 metaphases analyzed.
Result: 46,XX [20/20]

Comment: Normal female karyotype in all 20 metaphases analyzed. Notably, the del(7q) clone that was present in 2/20 metaphases at diagnosis (residual MDS-related clone) is no longer detectable in this evaluation, consistent with successful donor engraftment replacing the recipient's abnormal hematopoietic clone. No cytogenetic evidence of relapse or new clonal evolution.

VI. FLUORESCENCE IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (FISH) PANEL (Specimen C)

Probes applied: FLT3 locus (13q12), 7q31 (del7q probe), CEP7, chromosome 5 panel, MLL/KMT2A (11q23)
- FLT3-ITD FISH (13q12): Normal signal pattern. No FLT3 structural rearrangement. Note: FLT3-ITD is a small insertion mutation not detectable by standard FISH; confirmatory molecular testing performed (see below).
- del(7q) FISH (7q31/CEP7): NEGATIVE for del(7q). Previously identified del(7q) clone (2/20 metaphases at diagnosis) not detected. (Analyzed 200 interphase nuclei; 0% showing 7q31 signal loss, threshold for positivity: >10%).

VII. MOLECULAR STUDIES (Specimen C)

a) FLT3-ITD Quantitative PCR
Method: Fragment length analysis (capillary electrophoresis) and quantitative real-time PCR for FLT3-ITD.
Result: FLT3-ITD NOT DETECTED.
Sensitivity: Approximately 0.1% (1 in 1,000 cells) by standard fragment analysis; digital PCR sensitivity <0.01%.
Comment: The FLT3-ITD mutation (previously confirmed at diagnosis: exon 14-15 junction, insert length 39 bp, allelic ratio 0.82) is not detected in the current specimen. This represents clearance of the defining leukemic driver mutation.

b) DNMT3A p.R882H Digital Droplet PCR (ddPCR)
Method: Droplet digital PCR using allele-specific primer/probe set targeting DNMT3A c.2645G>A (p.R882H).
Result: NOT DETECTED.
Sensitivity: <0.01% VAF.
Comment: DNMT3A p.R882H, previously detected at VAF 44% at diagnosis, is not detected above the assay sensitivity threshold. Note: Given the concern for clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) persistence, repeat ddPCR for DNMT3A at day +180 and beyond is recommended per institutional post-transplant molecular surveillance protocol.

c) Additional molecular studies (SRSF2 p.P95H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, RUNX1 p.R204Q by ddPCR): PENDING at time of this report; results to be addended.

VIII. DONOR CHIMERISM BY STR ANALYSIS (Specimens C and D)

Method: Short tandem repeat (STR) multiplex PCR using 16 informative loci; donor and recipient pre-transplant reference samples available for comparison. Lineage-specific chimerism performed on sorted cell populations.

Results:
- Whole bone marrow chimerism: 98% donor, 2% recipient
- T-cell subset chimerism (CD3+ sorted): 95% donor, 5% recipient
- Myeloid subset chimerism (CD33+ sorted): 99% donor, 1% recipient
- Peripheral blood chimerism: 97% donor, 3% recipient

Comment: Findings are consistent with near-complete donor engraftment at day +30 post-alloHSCT. Residual low-level recipient chimerism in the T-cell compartment (5%) is within expected range for day +30 following RIC conditioning with ATG, and does not indicate graft failure or impending rejection. Mixed T-cell chimerism at this early time point is commonly observed after RIC regimens, particularly with ATG-containing GVHD prophylaxis, and is expected to continue converting toward full donor chimerism over subsequent months. Sequential chimerism monitoring at day +60, +90, and +180 is recommended per protocol.

No evidence of increasing recipient contribution that would suggest impending graft failure or leukemic relapse of host origin.

---

IMPRESSION / PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS

1. BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE (Day +30 post-matched unrelated donor alloHSCT): Normocellular to mildly hypercellular reconstituting marrow (50% cellularity) with trilineage hematopoiesis and normal maturation. No morphologic evidence of residual or relapsed acute myeloid leukemia. Myeloblasts < 2% by morphology and flow cytometry.

2. MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) BY MULTIPARAMETER FLOW CYTOMETRY: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%). No leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected.

3. CYTOGENETICS: 46,XX [20/20]. Normal female karyotype in all metaphases. del(7q) clone no longer detectable.

4. MOLECULAR MRD: FLT3-ITD NOT DETECTED. DNMT3A p.R882H NOT DETECTED. Additional somatic mutation ddPCR studies PENDING (to be addended).

5. DONOR CHIMERISM: Near-complete donor engraftment documented. Whole marrow 98% donor; myeloid 99% donor; T-cell 95% donor. Findings consistent with successful engraftment following RIC alloHSCT. Low-level mixed T-cell chimerism (5% recipient) within expected range for day +30 after ATG-containing RIC conditioning.

OVERALL CONCLUSION: Complete morphologic and immunophenotypic remission with MRD negativity and successful full donor engraftment confirmed at day +30 post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. No evidence of graft failure or AML relapse.

---

Addendum (PENDING): Results for SRSF2 p.P95H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, and RUNX1 p.R204Q ddPCR assays are pending and will be reported under a separate addendum upon completion.

---

Reporting Pathologist: [PATHOLOGIST NAME REDACTED], MD, PhD
Department of Hematopathology
Electronic signature applied: [DATE REDACTED]

This report has been reviewed by a board-certified hematopathologist. Clinical correlation recommended.
